preamble: The transformation from the voiced retroflex lateral approximant /ɭ/ to the voiced palatal approximant /j/ is not typically a direct, complete substitution. Instead, it often manifests as a phonetic realization where the retroflex lateral approximant is produced with a significant palatal glide component, or as part of a sequence that includes a palatal approximant. This process involves a shift in the place of articulation from retroflex to palatal, often accompanied by a modification or loss of the distinct lateral quality of the original retroflex sound.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, vocalization, loss of retroflexion, emergence of palatal approximant

examples.note: The retroflex lateral approximant /ɭ/ is realized as a sequence of palatal glide plus alveolar lateral in certain environments. For instance, the word 'arlenge' [ɐɭəŋə] might be pronounced [ɛjləŋə].

sources: Bowern, C. (2016). An acoustic study of multiple lateral consonants in three Central Australian languages. Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 46(1), 1-25.
